name: station_relative_approach_dir

symptoms: After navigating to a new base station, a kinematically converged grasp (err ~4e-4 m) misses physically — palm rakes the object sideways during descent
Pick lift is 0 cm; object is pushed laterally instead of lifted
trace_evidence: approach-direction helper had a hardcoded origin (base_xy=(-0.35, 0)) valid only for the original fixed-base layout
From any other station the tilted approach leans from the wrong side
Object pushed 6.6 cm horizontally in the direction opposite the wrong lean

when_to_apply: Any manipulation that uses a tilted or angled approach direction with a mobile base, or any helper that computes approach vectors from a fixed reference position rather than the live arm-base position.
repair_strategy: Always compute the approach direction's horizontal component from the robot's ACTUAL current arm-base position (FK query at plan time), both when scoring candidate stations and when planning the manipulation. Pass the live arm-base xy to desired_approach_dir() and any station-scoring heuristic instead of hardcoding a home position.
code_sketch: # Also fix station scoring so it uses live arm position:
validated_on: AlohaMini Pro (ManiSkill GPU sim) 2026-07-01: pick lift went from 0 cm (object pushed 6.6 cm sideways) to +11 cm lift after passing live arm_base_xy to approach helper
